Acacia peregrina is a species of thorny shrub or tree in the pea family, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The health benefits of Acacia peregrina include its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues, skin problems, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Acacia peregrina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and condensed tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acacia peregrina include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
